etymology.description: 豆(豆) (dòu, "bean") refers to the seed of leguminous plants, commonly used for consumption. 豉(豉) (chǐ, "fermented beans") specifically refers to fermented black soybeans, a staple ingredient in Chinese cooking.
etymology.interpretation: 豆(豆) (dòu, "bean") emphasizes the primary substance being beans. 豉(豉) (chǐ, "fermented beans") speaks to the process and transformation into a preserved, salty form useful in cooking. Together, 豆豉 symbolizes a specific food product that results from the fermentation process where beans become an umami-rich seasoning. The concept of transformation through fermentation often leads to ingredients associated with deep flavors, hence its culinary use in adding depth to dishes.

examples.chinese: 豆豉(豆豉)是一种常见的调味料。
examples.pinyin: Dòuchǐ shì yī zhǒng chángjiàn de tiáowèiliào.
examples.english: Fermented black beans are a common seasoning.

examples.chinese: 我们在菜里加了豆豉(我們在菜裡加了豆豉)，味道更加丰富(味道更加豐富)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen zài cài lǐ jiāle dòuchǐ, wèidào gèng jiā fēngfù.
examples.english: We added fermented black beans to the dish, making the flavor richer.

examples.chinese: 炒牛肉时(炒牛肉時)，豆豉可以提鲜(豆豉可以提鮮)。
examples.pinyin: Chǎo niúròu shí, dòuchǐ kěyǐ tíxiān.
examples.english: When stir-frying beef, fermented black beans can enhance the flavor.
